name: Cappucino Divinity
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine the sugar, dark Karo syrup, water, and salt.
2. Stirring constantly, bring to a boil over medium heat.
3. Reduce heat. Without stirring, cook until the thermometer reaches 248°F (soft ball stage).
4. Just before reaching 248°F, in a large bowl with a mixer on high speed, beat egg whites until stiff peaks form.
5. With the mixer still on high speed, slowly pour about half of the hot mixture over the egg whites.
6. Cook the remaining syrup to 272°F or until a small amount of mixture dropped into very cold water separates into threads that are hard but not brittle.
7. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla and dark rum.
8. Slowly pour the hot syrup back into the bowl with constant beating.
9. After the last addition of syrup, beat in coffee powder and ground cinnamon. Continue beating until the mixture begins to lose its gloss and a small amount holds a soft peak when dropped from a spoon.
10. If the mixture becomes too stiff for the mixer, continue beating with a heavy wooden spoon.
11. Drop by teaspoonfuls onto wax paper.
12. Store in a tightly covered container at room temperature. Makes 1 1/2 pounds.
Note: If desired, 1 cup chopped walnuts or pecans may be stirred into the mixture with coffee powder and cinnamon.
